meta.title: General Surgery Practice Software | OR Efficiency & Post-Op RTM | clinIQ
meta.description: Complete guide to general surgery practice operations. OR turnover optimization. Surgical LOMN generation. Post-op RTM billing. Pre-op coordination.

hero.subhead: Your OR efficiency drives revenue — 15 minutes off turnover is $200K/year recovered. Your surgical authorizations need systematic LOMN generation. Your post-op patients need monitoring that's billable. clinIQ optimizes OR efficiency, automates authorization, and captures post-op RTM.

sections.content:
General surgery practices perform high volumes of procedures across diverse case types — cholecystectomy, hernia repair, colectomy, appendectomy, breast surgery, and more.

**The OR efficiency opportunity:**

OR time is finite and expensive. Every minute of turnover time, every delayed start, every case that runs long without visibility — that's lost capacity. A 15-minute improvement in average turnover across 200 OR days equals 50 hours of recovered capacity.

**The authorization burden:**

Many general surgery procedures require prior authorization, especially for elective cases. Hernia repair, cholecystectomy, bariatric surgery — each needs documentation of medical necessity.

**The post-op monitoring gap:**

Post-surgical patients have recovery periods ranging from days to weeks. They should be monitoring: pain, wound healing, activity progression, red flag symptoms. This monitoring qualifies for RTM.

70 surgical patients × $130/month × 2-month average recovery = significant RTM revenue.

General surgery case mix varies significantly in duration:

- Laparoscopic cholecystectomy: 45-90 minutes
- Inguinal hernia repair: 30-60 minutes
- Laparoscopic colectomy: 2-4 hours
- Breast lumpectomy: 30-60 minutes
- Open hernia with mesh: 60-90 minutes

Turnover time between cases is where efficiency is won or lost.

**Where time gets wasted:**

Room ready but next patient not in pre-op. Pre-op ready but consent not signed. Anesthesia hasn't cleared the patient. Equipment not confirmed in room.

**What clinIQ does for OR efficiency:**

Room status visibility: Each OR — in procedure, turnover, ready. Estimated time remaining for current case.

Pre-op sync: Is the next patient in pre-op? Consent signed? Anesthesia cleared? Visible to OR team.

Turnover tracking: Case end → room ready → next patient in. Every turnover timestamped and measured.

First case on-time: 7:30 AM case starts at 7:30 AM. Delays tracked, causes identified.

**The math:**

15 minutes saved per OR day × 200 OR days = 50 hours recovered.

At $3,000/OR hour: $150,000-200,000 annual capacity recovered.

Many general surgery procedures require prior authorization:

**Commonly authorized procedures:**
- Cholecystectomy (especially elective)
- Hernia repair (inguinal, ventral, incisional)
- Bariatric surgery (extensive requirements)
- Breast surgery (reconstruction, reduction)
- Colectomy and bowel resection

**Common denial reasons:**

Medical necessity not established: Documentation doesn't show why surgery is needed now.

Conservative care not attempted: For some conditions, payers require trial of non-surgical management.

Incomplete documentation: Missing imaging, labs, or clinical findings.

**What clinIQ does:**

Auth tracking: Every surgical case tracked from scheduling through authorization. Pending, submitted, approved, denied, appealed.

Expiration alerts: Auth expires in X days, surgery not yet scheduled.

Denial management: Denial reason captured, appeal initiated, peer-to-peer scheduled.

Letters of Medical Necessity for general surgery require procedure-specific documentation.

**What a surgical LOMN requires:**

Patient presentation: Symptoms, duration, impact on function.

Physical findings: Exam findings supporting surgical indication.

Diagnostic workup: Imaging, labs, other studies.

Conservative treatment: Prior non-surgical management attempts (if applicable).

Medical necessity statement: Why surgery is indicated now.

**What clinIQ does:**

Procedure-specific templates: Cholecystectomy, hernia repair, colectomy, breast surgery — each has a template.

Auto-population: Patient demographics, diagnosis, imaging findings, clinical presentation pulled from documented data.

Surgeon workflow: Template loads populated. Surgeon reviews, edits, signs. 5 minutes instead of 30.

**The template library includes:**

Biliary: Cholecystectomy (acute, chronic, symptomatic cholelithiasis)

Hernia: Inguinal, ventral, incisional, umbilical, hiatal

Colorectal: Colectomy, appendectomy, hemorrhoidectomy

Breast: Lumpectomy, mastectomy, reconstruction, reduction

Bariatric: Gastric bypass, sleeve gastrectomy (extensive documentation)

Surgical patients require extensive pre-operative preparation:

**Pre-op requirements:**

Medical clearance: Cardiac clearance for high-risk patients, pulmonary clearance for COPD, diabetes optimization.

Labs: CBC, CMP, coagulation studies, type and screen. Timing requirements vary by payer.

Imaging: Current imaging supporting surgical indication.

Bowel prep: For colorectal cases, prep instructions and compliance.

Anesthesia: Pre-op anesthesia evaluation.

**What clinIQ does:**

Pre-op checklist: Every item tracked with status and responsible party.

Timeline visibility: Surgery in 7 days, 3 items incomplete. Visible to coordinators.

Automated alerts: 'Medical clearance not received, surgery in 5 days.'

Day-of verification: All items confirmed complete before patient arrives.

Post-surgical general surgery patients have recovery periods requiring monitoring.

**What post-op patients track:**

Pain: Daily pain levels, medication use, trending toward improvement.

Wound: Incision appearance, signs of infection, drainage.

Activity: Progression per post-op instructions, return to normal activities.

Bowel function: For abdominal surgery, return of bowel function.

Red flags: Fever, increasing pain, wound changes, concerning symptoms.

**The billing:**

CPT 98980: RTM treatment management, first 20 minutes. ~$50-70/month.
CPT 98981: Additional 20-minute increments.

With device supply codes: $100-130/patient/month.

Typical post-op monitoring: 2-4 weeks for minor procedures, 6-8 weeks for major.

**The math:**

70 surgical patients × $130/month × 2 months average = $18,200/quarter = $72,800/year.

**What clinIQ does:**

Post-op patient app: Recovery tracking specific to procedure type.

Milestone monitoring: Expected vs actual recovery progress.

Alert thresholds: Red flag symptoms trigger immediate alerts.

Automatic time tracking: Documentation of review time toward billing threshold.

General surgery implementation covers OR efficiency, authorization, and post-op monitoring.

**Week 1: Configuration**

We map your workflow: surgical scheduling, pre-op coordination, OR integration, post-op follow-up.

LOMN templates configured for your common procedures.

**Week 2: Training**

Surgical coordinators learn auth tracking and LOMN generation. Pre-op staff learn checklist management. Clinic staff learn post-op RTM enrollment.

**Week 3: Go-Live**

Live operation with support. LOMN generation active. RTM enrollment beginning.

**ROI Timeline:**

Month 1: LOMN time savings immediate. Pre-op visibility active.

Month 3: Authorization approval rates improving. RTM billing at scale.

Month 6: Full optimization. OR turnover data driving improvement.

**ROI Math:**

OR efficiency: $150,000-200,000/year capacity recovered

LOMN time savings: 15+ hours/month recovered

Post-op RTM: $70,000-90,000/year

**Investment:**

Professional: $499/month

Implementation: $750 one-time

faqs.q: How do you track OR turnover?
faqs.a: Case end, room ready, next patient in — each timestamped. Turnover duration calculated automatically. Analytics by surgeon, by case type, by day.

faqs.q: What LOMN templates do you have for general surgery?
faqs.a: Cholecystectomy, hernia repair (all types), colectomy, breast surgery, bariatric surgery, and more. Each with payer-specific language.

faqs.q: Is post-op monitoring billable as RTM?
faqs.a: Yes. CPT 98980/98981. Pain tracking, wound monitoring, activity progression — reviewed by your team. Billable during recovery period.

faqs.q: Does this work with surgery centers (ASCs)?
faqs.a: Yes. Pre-op coordination and authorization tracking work regardless of surgery location. OR turnover tracking requires integration with the surgical facility.
Emergency cases skip authorization workflow. Post-op RTM still applies for recovery monitoring.

faqs.q: How do you handle bariatric surgery authorization?
faqs.a: Bariatric has extensive requirements — supervised diet, psych eval, comorbidity documentation. Templates include all required elements for major payers.

testimonial.quote: OR turnover visibility changed everything. We see where time goes and can actually fix it. Plus the post-op RTM is pure incremental revenue.
